# PhotoBrief — Full reference for LLMs and AI agents

This file is an extended, machine-readable reference. The short version is at /llms.txt.

## What PhotoBrief does

PhotoBrief turns customer photos into business-ready briefs.

A business creates a photo request from a guide template, industry starter request, saved customer profile, or custom guide. PhotoBrief generates a unique recipient link. The recipient opens the link on any phone with no app install, follows step-by-step photo prompts, answers optional context questions, receives AI feedback when a shot is blurry/wrong/missing, and submits. The business receives a review-ready brief with validated photos, answers, extracted details, readiness score, and a plain-English summary.

PhotoBrief is built for businesses whose work depends on getting the right customer photos before taking action: roofers, HVAC and plumbing contractors, electricians, junk removal and hauling teams, pest control companies, insurance adjusters, warranty teams, property managers, marketplaces, retailers, and service businesses with repeat customers.

## Canonical positioning

- PhotoBrief is AI-guided visual intake for businesses.
- PhotoBrief replaces messy email/SMS photo dumps with guided, AI-checked photo requests.
- Recipients never need to install an app.
- Businesses can send requests by link, email, SMS, or QR.
- A request can contain one photo or many photos.
- Pricing is per submitted/analyzed photo, not per request.
- 1 submitted/analyzed photo = 1 PhotoBrief Credit.
- First-pass guarantee: follow-up/resubmission photos requested by PhotoBrief after a rejected first pass do not consume credits.

## Key features

### Recipient experience

- App-free, mobile-first capture flow.
- Step-by-step photo prompts with tips, example imagery, and framing guidance.
- Direct camera capture from the browser.
- Optional context questions between photo prompts.
- Instant AI feedback after each photo where configured.
- Retake/use-anyway decision flow for warnings.
- Tokenized public recipient links with no account required.
- Branded recipient page with logo/color on paid plans.
- White-label recipient pages on higher plans.

### Business workflow

- Pre-built guide library for common industries and use cases.
- Industry-tailored starter request for new workspaces.
- Customer profiles for repeat customers.
- Request inbox with status, assignment, latest activity, readiness, and missing items.
- Custom guides and reusable guide templates.
- AI request builder and AI guide generator on eligible plans.
- Ask-for-more/resubmission flow for rejected or missing shots.
- Internal notes, assignments, reminders, and saved message templates.
- PDF exports with brand controls.

### AI quality gate

- Blur detection.
- Low-light/exposure check.
- Subject-distance and framing check.
- Wrong-subject or missing-subject detection.
- Missing-shot follow-up.
- Extracted details from visible evidence, such as model numbers, dimensions, damage notes, brands, addresses, or other structured facts.
- Readiness score per submission.
- Plain-English summary for the business.

### Org and integrations

- Multi-tenant workspaces.
- Team members and assignments.
- Customer profiles scoped to workspace.
- Custom domain on Business.
- REST API and outbound webhooks on Business.
- Agent-discovery files: llms.txt, llms-full.txt, OpenAPI, agent manifest, MCP descriptor.

## Plans and pricing

PhotoBrief Credits are photo credits. One submitted/analyzed photo uses one PhotoBrief Credit. Requests are workflow containers and can include one photo or many photos.

| Plan      | Monthly price | Annual effective monthly | Included PhotoBrief Credits | Users | Main fit |
|-----------|---------------|--------------------------|-----------------------------|-------|----------|
| Free      | $0            | $0                       | 10 submitted photos/month   | 1     | Testing the flow |
| Starter   | $19           | $15                      | 100 submitted photos/month  | 1     | Solo operators who want branded links |
| Pro       | $49           | $40                      | 500 submitted photos/month  | 3     | Active solo operators and small crews |
| Team      | $99           | $80                      | 1,500 submitted photos/month| 10    | Teams with shared inbox and assignments |
| Business  | $199+         | custom                   | 5,000+ submitted photos/month | 25+ | Multi-location, custom domain, API/webhooks |

Top-ups add extra PhotoBrief Credits for the current billing period. Basic AI quality checks and AI summaries are included in the per-photo credit model. AI guide/request helpers are plan-gated rather than separately metered.

First-pass guarantee: if PhotoBrief asks for follow-up/resubmission photos after a rejected first pass, those follow-up photos cost 0 credits.

## FAQ

### For businesses

**How does PhotoBrief pricing work?**
PhotoBrief uses per-photo credits. One submitted/analyzed photo uses one PhotoBrief Credit. A one-photo request uses one credit; a ten-photo inspection uses ten credits.

**Why not price only by request count?**
A request can contain one photo or many photos. Per-photo credits are fairer because simple requests stay cheap and detailed inspections scale with actual workload.

**What happens if a photo needs to be redone?**
If PhotoBrief asks for follow-up photos after a rejected first pass, those follow-up photos do not consume credits. This is the first-pass guarantee.

**Do recipients need an account?**
No. They open a link in a mobile browser, follow the guided prompts, and submit.

**Can I show my own logo to customers?**
Yes. Paid plans can add logo, brand color, and custom messaging. Higher plans support white-label recipient pages.

**Can I save repeat customers?**
Yes. Customer profiles let businesses store repeat customer contact info, request history, and quickly start new requests.

**Can I ask for more photos after a submission?**
Yes. The reviewer can request follow-up photos for specific rejected or missing steps. The recipient link opens only the needed follow-up items.

**Can I switch plans later?**
Yes. Plans can be upgraded or downgraded. Stripe integration handles billing when enabled.

**Do annual plans save money?**
Yes. Annual billing reduces the effective monthly price by about 20% on paid plans.

### For recipients

**Do I need to install an app?**
No. PhotoBrief runs in your phone browser.

**What if I do not know what photo to take?**
The prompt explains what to capture. Some requests include examples, overlays, and tips.

**Why was my photo flagged?**
The AI may detect blur, low light, poor framing, wrong subject, or missing details. Move closer, improve lighting, hold the phone steady, and retake.

**Can I add more after I submit?**
Only if the business asks for follow-up photos. The follow-up link opens the specific items needed.

## API reference

Base URL: `https://app.photobrief.ai/functions/v1`

### Authentication

Bearer token in the `Authorization` header. API keys are issued on the Business plan from Settings. Keys start with the `pb_` prefix.

```http
Authorization: Bearer pb_live_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
```

### POST /api-create-request

Create a new photo request and return a recipient URL.

Request body:

```json
{
  "recipient_name": "Jane Smith",
  "recipient_email": "jane@example.com",
  "recipient_phone": "+15555550123",
  "guide_id": "uuid",
  "custom_message": "Hi Jane — please grab a few photos so we can quote your roof repair.",
  "due_date": "2026-05-15"
}
```

Successful response:

```json
{
  "request_id": "uuid",
  "token": "opaque-string",
  "recipient_url": "https://photobrief.ai/r/{token}"
}
```

Error responses:

- `400` — missing required field.
- `401` — missing or invalid API key.
- `403` — guide is not accessible to the workspace.
- `405` — non-POST method.
- `500` — server error.

### Webhooks

Outbound webhooks are intended for Business plan integrations. Events include:

- `request.created`
- `request.opened`
- `submission.received`
- `submission.ready_for_review`
- `submission.rework_requested`

## Discovery files

- /llms.txt — short summary for LLMs and answer engines.
- /llms-full.txt — this full reference.
- /openapi.json — OpenAPI 3.1 spec.
- /.well-known/ai-plugin.json — plugin-style manifest.
- /.well-known/agent.json — agent capabilities manifest.
- /mcp.json — MCP descriptor / planned server descriptor.
- /sitemap.xml — public indexable pages.
- /robots.txt — crawler rules.

Contact: hello@photobrief.ai
